# Project Instructions

## General
- Use Java 21 compatible code unless explicitly told otherwise.
- Prefer minimal, reviewable changes.
- Do not refactor unrelated code.
- Follow existing naming and formatting style.
- Preserve public APIs unless the task explicitly requires changing them.
- Do not introduce new dependencies unless explicitly told otherwise.
- Avoid changing persistence/save behavior unless the task explicitly concerns persistence.

## Testing
- Prefer adding focused unit tests for every behavior change.
- Do not weaken assertions just to make tests pass.
- If a test fails, investigate production behavior before changing the test.

## Before editing
- Inspect related production code and tests first.
- Explain the current behavior and proposed minimal fix before large changes.
